heading: Why trust this LA Metro guide
intro: This guide is built from real Metro use across the tourist-relevant lines in Los Angeles — understanding what works, what does not, and when the Metro genuinely saves you time and money.

items.title: Honest about Metro's limitations
items.text: The LA Metro is more useful than its reputation for specific routes — but it cannot replace a car for most of LA. This guide tells you exactly which lines are worth taking and which routes still require Uber.

items.title: Tourist-focused line coverage
items.text: The B Line to Hollywood and E Line to Santa Monica are the two lines that matter most for visitors. This guide covers them in depth and explains when they are worth the hassle.

items.title: Cost comparison included
items.text: Metro fare ($1.75) vs. parking ($20–40) vs. Uber ($15–25) — the guide gives you the honest breakdown so you can make the right call for each trip.

items.title: Safety covered
items.lead: When and where it is safe:
items.text: The LA Metro has a mixed safety reputation. This guide tells you honestly which lines and times are comfortable and when rideshare is the better choice.

footer: If you want to use the LA Metro intelligently as a tourist — knowing when it saves you money and when Uber is the right call — this guide covers it.
